If a broken cloud presents the appearance of an armed person
and is situated close to the Sun, there will be danger to the king
from enemies. If a white aerial city (formed by clouds) is approach-
ed by the Sun, the besieged town will be victorious; while if it is
cut into two by the Sun, the town will be destroyed.
[Bhattotpala interprets the second line of the verse differently:
is rendered by him as the aerial city 'by which the Sun is
attacked', but the natural interpretation would be—'which is attack-
ed by the Sun*. For, we know that a city is attacked or besieged
by an enemy king. 5^TT*T: ^so rendered differently by him :
5* This is a far-fetched construction. Here
5T?n*T simply means, the victory of the defenders of the city. In the

Indications at Dawn and Twilight XXX 291
